originalText: The article states that global CO2 emissions dropped by 25% in 2023 compared to 2019 levels. It also claims that 90% of this reduction came from the aviation sector alone, and that the Arctic sea ice fully recovered to levels observed in the 1980s.

claims.claimText: global CO2 emissions dropped by 25% in 2023 compared to 2019 levels
90% of this reduction came from the aviation sector alone
Arctic sea ice fully recovered to levels observed in the 1980s

sources.name: IEA Emissions Report 2023
sources.evidence: Reports a smaller reduction concentrated in specific sectors.

sources.name: News Blog
sources.evidence: Claims dramatic 25% drop without citing underlying data.

verificationResults.summary: Magnitude of global reduction is exaggerated and not consistently reported.

sources.name: ICAO Aviation Emissions Factsheet
sources.evidence: Aviation accounts for a much smaller share of global CO2 reduction.

sources.name: NSIDC Arctic Sea Ice Data
sources.evidence: No evidence of full recovery to 1980s ice extent.
